identity.short_name: Laplace ODE \to denominator roots \to set-sum of Re+2Im mod 1000

problem_spec.summary: Solve a constant-coefficient IVP via Laplace transform to obtain f(s) as a rational function. Extract the denominator polynomial in lowest terms, list its complex roots with multiplicity, map each root to Re(r)+2Im(r), deduplicate to a set, sum, scale, take absolute value, and reduce mod 1000.
givens: y satisfies y''(x)+2y'(x)+y(x)=e^{-3x}\cos(2x) with y(0)=0 and y'(0)=0.
P(s) is the denominator of f(s) in lowest terms.
r_1,...,r_n are the complex roots of P(s) counted with multiplicity.
ask: Determine the residue class of M modulo 1000 (representative in {0,...,999}).

parameter(s).name: forcing
parameter(s).purpose: ensures Laplace transform yields shifted rational terms (here e^{-3x}cos(2x))

parameter(s).name: characteristic_coeffs
parameter(s).purpose: determines the homogeneous factor of the denominator (here y''+2y'+y)

parameter(s).name: scaling_factor
parameter(s).purpose: multiplies the set-sum before absolute value and mod reduction (here 50)

parameter(s).name: set_map
parameter(s).purpose: defines the invariant extracted from each root

invariant(s).name: denominator_from_transfer_and_forcing
invariant(s).statement: For constant-coefficient IVPs solved by Laplace transform, f(s) is rational with denominator given by the characteristic polynomial factors times forcing-shift factors (after simplification).

invariant(s).name: conjugate_pair_re_im_values
invariant(s).statement: Non-real roots occur in conjugate pairs; applying Re(r)+2Im(r) typically yields distinct real values unless a collision occurs, which is removed by set-deduplication.

essential_assumption(s): The denominator is taken after full simplification (lowest terms).
Roots are counted with multiplicity before applying the set map.
Re(z), Im(z) are the usual real and imaginary parts over ℂ.
uniqueness_certificate(s): The IVP uniquely determines y, hence uniquely determines f(s). Lowest-terms reduction fixes P(s) uniquely up to a unit scalar, which does not change its roots. The multiset of roots with multiplicity is unique; applying the map Re+2Im, set-deduplication, summation, scaling, absolute value, and mod reduction yields a unique integer residue class mod 1000.

technique(s).name: Laplace_IVP_to_rational
technique(s).purpose: convert the IVP to an algebraic equation for f(s)

technique(s).name: denominator_and_roots_extraction
technique(s).purpose: compute P(s) in lowest terms and its roots with multiplicity

technique(s).name: scale_abs_mod
technique(s).purpose: compute M=|scaling_factor * sum_S| and reduce mod modulus

theorem(s).name: roots_of_polynomial_with_real_coeffs
theorem(s).role: complex roots occur in conjugate pairs

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: convert differential equations to algebra via Laplace transform, then read invariants from the denominator roots

critical_trick(s): Use Laplace transform plus initial-data substitution to isolate f(s) as a rational function.
Identify the denominator factors (including repeated roots) and list roots with multiplicity.
Apply the Re+2Im map and set-deduplication before summing, then scale/abs/mod.

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: symbolic Laplace transforms, rational simplification, polynomial factorization, and exact root extraction

import_hint(s): from sympy import symbols, laplace_transform, factor, Poly

task(s).name: symbolic_verify_f
task(s).reason: compute f(s) from the IVP and simplify to lowest terms

task(s).name: extract_P_and_roots
task(s).reason: form P(s) from the denominator of f(s) and compute roots with multiplicity

task(s).name: compute_set_sum_and_mod
task(s).reason: map roots to Re+2Im, deduplicate, sum, scale, abs, and reduce mod 1000
